apply: Students will be able to apply various methods of data collection in real-life scenarios.
Students will be able to use tools and techniques for effective data collection.
Students will be able to design a data collection plan for a given problem.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the strengths and weaknesses of different data collection methods.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of data collection tools and techniques.
Students will be able to identify the potential biases in data collection and suggest ways to mitigate them.
remember: Students will be able to explain the meaning and significance of various sources of data.
Students will be able to recall the various tools used for collection of data.
Students will be able to identify the importance of primary and secondary data.
understand: Students will be able to understand the process of data collection and its limitations.
Students will be able to comprehend the role of various institutions in data collection.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of data collection on decision-making.

ncert_objectives: Explain the concept of data and its significance in economics.
Distinguish between primary and secondary data.
Describe the various tools and techniques used for data collection.
Explain the importance of data collection in decision-making.
Discuss the role of institutions in data collection.
board_exam_objectives: What is data? Explain different sources of data.
Distinguish between primary and secondary data.
Describe the methods of data collection.
